LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS DONNA DINAOPLI AND BRADY LAWRENCE'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT LYFT, INC. TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AND ESI AND TO PRODUCE PMQS FOR DEPOSITION IS GRANTED IN ITS ENTIRETY. LYFT APPEARS TO ACKNOWLEDGE THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE THE RIGHT TO THE DISCOVERY SOUGHT TO BE COMPELLED BY THIS MOTION. ABSENT AN AGREEMENT BY THE PARTIES, ALL DOCUMENTS AND ESI THAT ARE THE SUBJECT OF THIS MOTION NEED TO BE PRODUCED BY JANUARY 18 AND THE PMQ DEPOSITION MUST TAKE PLACE ON JANUARY 20. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/HK

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Defendant Lyft, Inc. To Produce Documents And Electronically Stored Information And To Produce Its Pmqs For Deposition
Off Calendar Jan-13-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiffs Donna Dinaopli and Brady Lawrence's motion to compel defendant Lyft, Inc. to produce documents and ESI and to produce PMQs for deposition is granted in its entirety. Lyft appears to acknowledge that plaintiffs have the right to the discovery sought to be compelled by this motion. Absent an agreement by the parties, all documents and ESI that are the subject of this motion need to be produced by January 18 and the PMQ deposition must take place on January 20. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

Off Calendar May-24-2016 Text Ruling

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Continued To Jun-15-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Donna Dinapolis And Brady Lawrences Motion To Compel Defendants Lyft, Inc.S And Shanti Adhikaris Further Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROSS J. PSYHOGOS OF THE JONES CLIFFORD LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-431-5310 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Donna Dinapolis And Brady Lawrences Motion To Compel Defendants Lyft, Inc.S And Shanti Adhikaris Further Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents is GRANTED in part. The parties appear to have engaged in an exchange of meet and confer correspondence that was somewhat constructive. Several issues remain, however, as referenced in the "final 'meet and confer' attempt" of plaintiff's counsel. Letter dated Feb. 1, 2016. That letter also referenced earlier correspondence dated Nov. 15, 2015 and Dec. 22, 2015. No substantive opposition to the motion was filed. Instead, defense counsel said that further responses, and verifications, would be served, so that the motion could be taken off calendar. That does not appear to have been done. That the action is not yet at issue as to all parties is immaterial to the need for timely compliance with the discovery served on Aug. 24, 2015. Accordingly, the motion is granted, save as to the request to order the delivery of a consent to obtain cell phone records and as to RFP 13 (Dinapoli to Adhikari) and RFP 14 (Lawrence to Adhikari). Those parts of the motion are DENIED as moot, without prejudice in the event the consent already delivered proves unsatisfactory to the intended purpose. Accordingly, by April 25, 2016, defendants shall serve (or, if the documents to be produced are voluminous, make available) further verified responses to form and special interrogatories, and to the requests for production, identified in the letter dated Feb. 1, 2016 from J. Kevin Morrison, and also produce responsive non-privileged documents. To the extent any defendant seeks to withhold a document on the ground of privilege, it shall clearly identify each such document and state the basis for the claimed privilege or protection with sufficient particularity to enable the court to sustain or overrule the claimed privilege or protection. See CCP Section 2031.240(c)(1). Said privilege log shall be served no later than April 29, 2016. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro tem; Charles Ragan Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.=302/jpt
